results.title: وزير الخارجية العماني: سياسة الاحتواء وهم.. والخليج بحاجة إلى نظام أمني جديد

results.title: الداخلية تضبط 9 متهمين باستغلال الأطفال في التسول بالجيزة
results.description: واصلت أجهزة وزارة الداخلية جهودها المستمرة على مدار الساعة، لمكافحة جرائم استغلال الأحداث، وتمكنت من 12 حدث خلال استغلالهم في أعمال التسول. وتمكنت الإدارة العامة لمباحث رعاية الأحداث بقطاع الشرطة المتخصصة من ضبط 7 رجال سيدتين (لاثنين منهم معلومات جنائية)، لقيامهم باستغلال الأطفال الأحداث في أعمال التسول واستجداء المارة وبيع السلع بطريقة إلحاحية بنطاق محافظة.
creator: الأسبوع

results.title: EU targets Sudan’s gold trade with new sanctions
results.description: EU targets Sudan’s gold trade with new sanctions The measures prohibit the purchase, import or transfer of gold originating in Sudan, while also banning the sale, supply, transfer or export to Sudan of mercury and cyanide. Tuesday 14/07/2026 Gold bars seized at Khartoum Airport are displayed in an investigation into possible smuggling, in Khartoum. By Iman Zayat • News •0 BRUSSELS The European Union imposed new sanctions on Sudan on Monday, banning imports of Sudanese gold and restricting exports of chemicals used in gold mining in an effort to curb financing for the country’s three-year civil war. The measures prohibit the purchase, import or transfer of gold originating in Sudan, while also banning the sale, supply, transfer or export to Sudan of mercury and cyanide, chemicals widely used in gold extraction. “The decision introduces a ban on the purchase, import or transfer of gold originating in Sudan. It also bans the sale, supply, transfer or export of mercury and cyanide to Sudan,” the Council of the European Union said in a statement. The EU said gold had become a major source of revenue sustaining the conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which has devastated the country since fighting erupted in April 2023. By restricting trade in Sudanese gold and limiting access to key mining chemicals, Brussels said it aimed to reduce the financial resources available to those fuelling the conflict. The sanctions include exemptions for humanitarian purposes, public health emergencies and disaster response, with restrictions on mercury and cyanide not applying in those circumstances. The EU described the measures as part of its broader response to what it called an unprecedented humanitarian crisis marked by widespread violations of international humanitarian and human rights law. The conflict, now in its fourth year, has displaced millions of people, devastated infrastructure and left large parts of Sudan facing severe food insecurity, making it one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises. The sanctions build on a framework of restrictive measures adopted by the EU in October 2023 against individuals and entities accused of undermining Sudan’s stability and political transition. The bloc has expanded those listings several times, most recently in January 2026. The EU has repeatedly called on both the SAF and the RSF to engage in ceasefire negotiations, allow rapid and unhindered humanitarian access, support an inclusive civilian-led political transition and uphold international law. Following the third International Conference on Sudan in Berlin in April, the EU reaffirmed its call for an immediate and lasting ceasefire and warned that external actors must stop fuelling the conflict. It also pledged to use all available diplomatic and economic tools, including additional sanctions targeting Sudan’s war economy, to press for peace. Monday’s measures mark the latest effort by Brussels to increase pressure on the warring parties by targeting the financial networks that sustain the conflict rather than focusing solely on military and political leaders. Suggested By Editor US escalates pressure on Sudan army over chemical weapons allegations Suggested By Editor US escalates pressure on Sudan army over chemical weapons allegations
